settings.text: Everything parents ask us most — about camps, programs, registration, and how DC Way runs day to day. Can't find your answer? Reach out on our contact page.

settings.heading: Who is DC Way for?
settings.row_content: Boys and girls ages 4–13 across the DC, MD, and VA area. Our programs are non-competitive and welcome players of every skill level — from absolute beginners to seasoned competitive players.

settings.heading: Where are your locations?
settings.row_content: We run programs across multiple Capitol Hill fields and DC-area sites. See the Locations list at the bottom of most pages for addresses and hours.

settings.heading: Do I need a membership to register?
settings.row_content: No — membership is optional. Anyone can register for camps, leagues, and clinics. Members simply receive a 10% discount on every program for a year. Learn about membership.

settings.heading: What do we offer at Summer Camp?
settings.row_content: Weekly soccer camps Monday through Friday in a fun, positive, non-competitive, safe environment. Kids grow technically and socially while staying active all summer long.

settings.heading: What ages can attend camp?
settings.row_content: Boys and girls ages 4–13, split into age- and skill-appropriate groups. 4-year-olds must be potty trained and able to follow directions.

settings.heading: What should my child bring?
settings.row_content: Soccer cleats or sneakers, shin guards, a refillable water bottle, sunscreen, a hat, and a packed lunch. We provide soccer balls and all equipment.

settings.heading: What are the camp options and pricing?
settings.row_content:
Full Week — 9:00 am to 3:00 pm.
Extended Full Week — 9:00 am to 5:30 pm with before- and after-care available. Pricing is listed on each camp page.

settings.heading: What's the difference between a camp and a program?
settings.row_content: Camps run for a full week (or single day) during school breaks. Programs — leagues, clinics, after-school, private lessons — run weekly throughout the school year.

settings.heading: Do programs require prior experience?
settings.row_content: No. Every program is open to first-timers as well as experienced players. We group players by age and skill so everyone gets the right level of challenge.

settings.heading: What if my child misses a session?
settings.row_content: We don't pro-rate for missed sessions, but coaches are happy to catch your player up on what they missed in the next class.

settings.heading: How do I register my child?
settings.row_content: Visit the Registration page, pick the camp or program, choose the week or session, and check out. You'll receive a confirmation email with everything you need.

settings.heading: What payment methods do you accept?
settings.row_content: All major credit and debit cards through our secure Shopify checkout, plus Shop Pay, Apple Pay, and Google Pay where supported.

settings.heading: What is the refund and transfer policy?
settings.row_content: Cancellations 14+ days before the start date are eligible for a full refund less a processing fee. Within 14 days, we'll transfer your spot to another week if availability allows. Contact us for help.

settings.heading: What if a camp or program is sold out?
settings.row_content: Get in touch on our contact page and we'll add you to the waitlist. Spots open up regularly as schedules change.

settings.text: We'd love to help. Reach out and we'll get back to you within one business day.
